_meta.description: Technology catalog for project scaffolding. Update via /agentops:tech-catalog. Categories are extensible — add new ones as needed.

options.name: SolidStart
options.description: SolidJS meta-framework, fine-grained reactivity, ~40% faster than React

options.name: TanStack Start
options.description: Full-stack React, SSR, streaming, server functions, type-safe

options.name: htmx
options.description: Hypermedia-driven, no JS build step, server-rendered, growing fast

options.name: Livewire
options.description: Laravel, reactive UI without JS, server-driven

options.name: Inertia.js
options.description: SPA experience with server-side routing, Laravel/Rails

options.name: Litestar
options.description: Python, 2x faster than FastAPI, batteries-included, msgspec

options.name: Effect
options.description: TypeScript, functional effect system, concurrency, error handling

options.name: Neon
options.description: Serverless Postgres, scale-to-zero, branching, acquired by Databricks

options.name: Valkey
options.description: Open-source Redis fork (Linux Foundation), AWS/Google-backed, Valkey 8.1 GA

options.name: PlanetScale
options.description: Managed MySQL, branching — free tier removed Apr 2024, min $40/mo

design_patterns.label: Design Patterns & Architecture
design_patterns.question: Which design patterns and architectural approaches apply?

options.name: Action-Based Architecture
options.description: One class per action, single __invoke method, replaces bloated controllers/services

options.name: Single Action Controllers
options.description: One route per controller class, __invoke(), focused responsibility

options.name: Domain-Driven Design (DDD)
options.description: Bounded contexts, aggregates, value objects, ubiquitous language, domain events

options.name: CQRS
options.description: Separate read/write models, dedicated query and command handlers

options.name: Event Sourcing
options.description: Persist state as immutable event stream, rebuild state by replay, full audit trail

options.name: CQRS + Event Sourcing
options.description: Combined pattern — commands produce events, projections build read models

options.name: Clean Architecture
options.description: Concentric layers, dependency rule inward, use cases, entities, interface adapters

options.name: Clean Code Principles
options.description: Meaningful names, small functions, no side effects, single level of abstraction

options.name: Hexagonal / Ports & Adapters
options.description: Core domain isolation, ports define contracts, adapters plug in infrastructure

options.name: Vertical Slice Architecture
options.description: Organise by feature not layer, each slice owns its full stack

options.name: Repository Pattern
options.description: Abstract data access behind interfaces, swap implementations

options.name: Service Layer
options.description: Business logic in dedicated service classes, thin controllers

options.name: Strategy Pattern
options.description: Interchangeable algorithms, runtime selection via interface

options.name: Observer / Event-Driven
options.description: Publish/subscribe, decoupled event handlers, Laravel events/listeners

options.name: Decorator Pattern
options.description: Wrap behaviour around existing objects, middleware chains

options.name: Pipeline / Chain of Responsibility
options.description: Sequential processing stages, middleware, Laravel Pipeline class

options.name: Saga Pattern
options.description: Distributed transactions, compensating actions, long-running processes

options.name: Circuit Breaker
options.description: Fault tolerance, graceful degradation, retry limits

options.name: ADR (Action-Domain-Responder)
options.description: Web-specific MVC refinement — action receives, domain processes, responder renders

options.name: Modular Monolith
options.description: Monolith with strict module boundaries, prepare for future extraction

options.name: Microservices
options.description: Independent deployable services, API boundaries, eventual consistency

options.name: Strangler Fig
options.description: Incremental migration, wrap legacy system, replace piece by piece

coding_standards.label: Coding Standards & Style
coding_standards.question: Which coding standards and style guides?

options.name: PEP 8
options.description: Python style guide, indentation, naming, imports

engineering_principles.label: Engineering Principles
engineering_principles.question: Which engineering principles guide this project?

options.name: SOLID
options.description: Single responsibility, open/closed, Liskov, interface segregation, dependency inversion

options.name: DRY
options.description: Don't Repeat Yourself — single source of truth for knowledge

options.name: KISS
options.description: Keep It Simple — simplest solution that works

options.name: YAGNI
options.description: You Aren't Gonna Need It — don't build for hypothetical futures

options.name: Composition over Inheritance
options.description: Favour object composition, traits, mixins over class hierarchies

options.name: Fail Fast
options.description: Detect and surface errors immediately, don't mask failures

options.name: Convention over Configuration
options.description: Sensible defaults, reduce boilerplate, Rails/Laravel philosophy

options.name: Defensive Programming
options.description: Validate inputs, handle edge cases, assert invariants

options.name: Immutability by Default
options.description: Prefer immutable data, explicit mutation points

options.name: Trunk-Based Development
options.description: Short-lived branches, frequent integration, feature flags

options.name: Continuous Integration
options.description: Automated build/test on every push, fast feedback

options.name: Continuous Deployment
options.description: Automated production releases, feature flags for safety

options.name: Twelve-Factor App
options.description: Config in env, stateless processes, disposability, logs as streams

options.name: Zero Trust
options.description: Never trust, always verify, least privilege, micro-segmentation

options.name: Shift Left Security
options.description: Security in dev, not just ops — SAST, DAST, dependency scanning early

options.name: Observability-First
options.description: Structured logging, distributed tracing, metrics from day one

observability.label: Observability & Monitoring
observability.question: Which observability tools and services?
observability.appliesWhen: project needs monitoring, logging, tracing, or error tracking

options.name: OpenTelemetry
options.description: Vendor-neutral standard for metrics, traces, logs, profiles — 79% adoption

options.name: Sentry
options.description: Error tracking, performance monitoring, distributed tracing, session replay

options.name: Axiom
options.description: Machine data platform, OTel metrics at petabyte scale, AI observability SDK

options.name: Grafana + Loki + Tempo
options.description: Open-source dashboards, log aggregation, distributed tracing

options.name: Datadog
options.description: Full-stack observability, APM, logs, infrastructure, RUM

options.name: New Relic
options.description: Full-stack APM, logs, infrastructure, browser monitoring

options.name: Baselime (Cloudflare)
options.description: Serverless observability, acquired by Cloudflare, free, OTel-native

options.name: Highlight.io
options.description: Full-stack monitoring, frontend + backend pairing, session replay

options.name: Laravel Pulse
options.description: Laravel real-time application performance monitoring dashboard
